notebook_id: N/A — web search unavailable, research synthesized from industry knowledge
research_method: Industry knowledge synthesis (web search unavailable due to API configuration issue)
competitors_analyzed: jilsander.com — minimalist luxury, similar aesthetic to Entono
cosstores.com — affordable minimalist fashion, editorial content structure
the-row.com — ultra-luxury minimalist, sparse content, image-heavy
lemaire.fr — French minimalist luxury, poetic brand voice
marineserre.com — avant-garde, crescent moon motif, editorial lookbook
navigation_patterns: Minimal top nav with 3-5 links — Entono's 4 links (Collections, Lookbook, About, Contact) aligns with industry standard
Hamburger menu on mobile is universal — Entono's implementation with animated transitions matches luxury sites
Fixed/sticky header with transparency-to-solid transition is standard on editorial sites (Jil Sander, The Row)
Single-page anchor navigation is common for brand showcase sites — Entono uses this correctly
Avoid: mega-menus, category drop-downs, search bars — Entono correctly omits these (no e-commerce)
hero_patterns: Full-viewport hero with editorial image + brand name is the dominant pattern — Entono follows this
Parallax scrolling on hero image is common on luxury sites (Lemaire, Khaite) — Entono implements this
Tagline/subtitle below brand name is standard — Entono uses 'The depth of hue. The rhythm of form.' effectively
Scroll indicator at bottom of hero is near-universal — Entono uses a minimalist line animation
Avoid: video backgrounds on hero (heavy, slow load) — Entono correctly uses static image with parallax
Avoid: CTA buttons on hero (luxury sites rarely have 'Shop Now' — Entono correctly omits)
content_structure: Hero → Collections → Lookbook → About → Contact is a proven structure for fashion brand showcase sites
Collections presented as editorial cards with hover effects — standard on sites like Acne Studios, Toteme
Lookbook as masonry grid with lightbox — common pattern (Marine Serre, Khaite)
About section with brand story + stats — Entono's 3-paragraph philosophy + stats grid matches industry
Contact as footer-only is common, but Entono's dedicated contact section adds form interaction
Avoid: blog/content sections on brand-only sites — Entono correctly omits
Avoid: social media feeds — Entono correctly omits (luxury sites rarely embed live feeds)
cta_patterns: Luxury fashion sites rarely have CTAs — Entono correctly omits 'Shop Now' / 'Buy' buttons
Contact form is the primary interaction — Entono implements this with elegant underline inputs
Hover-only interactions (no click required) are standard for editorial content — Entono uses this on collections
Lightbox interaction for lookbook images is standard — Entono implements with Framer Motion scale animation
Avoid: email signup popups — Entono correctly omits (luxury sites don't do aggressive capture)
differentiation_opportunities: Autumn-only focus is unique — no major competitor centers exclusively on one season. This is Entono's strongest differentiator.
Autumn as a color philosophy (not just season) — Entono's brand voice ('The depth of hue') is more poetic than competitors
Single-page cinematic scroll experience — most fashion sites use multi-page navigation. Entono's scroll-driven narrative is more immersive
Warm earth tone palette vs. industry-standard black/white — Entono's oat + charcoal + sienna is distinctive
Playfair Display serif + warm tones creates a warmer, more inviting feel than the ultra-cool minimalism of Jil Sander / The Row
The '1 Season' stat in About section reinforces the niche focus — a unique storytelling element
visual_patterns_to_avoid: Black and white only — Entono's warm earth tones already differentiate
Heavy video content — slow load, poor mobile experience
Aggressive animations — luxury sites favor restrained motion. Entono's subtle Framer Motion transitions are appropriate
Grid-heavy e-commerce layouts — Entono uses editorial masonry, which is more appropriate
Sans-serif only typography — Entono's Playfair Display serif is correct for luxury positioning
Bright accent colors — Entono stays within warm earth tones, matching luxury standards
Pop-up modals and email captures — Entono correctly omits these
technical_recommendations: Image optimization is critical — Entono's 7 images total ~10MB. Use Next.js Image component or manual optimization (WebP, lazy loading) to ensure fast load
Framer Motion is the right choice — industry standard for luxury site animations. Entono uses it correctly
Tailwind CSS v4 is appropriate — matches modern luxury sites. Entono's @theme inline tokens are well-structured
Consider adding Open Graph / Twitter Card meta tags for social sharing (most fashion sites have rich previews)
Consider adding structured data (JSON-LD) for brand SEO — Entono currently has basic metadata only
Consider adding a favicon/brand icon — Entono may not have one yet
